issues.description: Listing 'the function blows up to infinity' flatly as a case where the limit 'does not exist' is slightly imprecise relative to AP conventions. AP distinguishes a true two-sided infinite limit (written lim = +infinity or -infinity, where both sides agree in sign) from DNE. Saying it 'DNE' is acceptable shorthand but glosses over the case where students are expected to write the answer as +/- infinity rather than just 'DNE'.
issues.fix: Add a half-sentence clarifying: 'When both sides go to the same infinity we usually write the answer as plus or minus infinity to describe the behavior; the finite limit still does not exist.'

issues.description: States there are 'three flavors of discontinuity' (removable, jump, infinite). Oscillating discontinuity (e.g. sin(1/x) at 0), which the script itself invokes in 07_dne, is a genuine fourth type and creates an internal inconsistency — the oscillation case from 07_dne does not fit any of the three named flavors.
issues.fix: Either say 'the three most common AP-tested flavors' or briefly acknowledge oscillating/essential discontinuity to stay consistent with the sin(1/x) example used earlier.

issues.description: The module is titled 'Limits and Continuity' and the task brief covers all of Unit 1 (limit laws, algebraic manipulation, squeeze theorem, infinite limits & vertical asymptotes, limits at infinity & horizontal asymptotes, IVT). This video covers only limit definition/notation, one-sided limits, DNE, continuity test, and discontinuity types. The script honestly flags itself as an overview (03_overview, 14_path) and defers limit laws/squeeze theorem to 'Module Two', so this is disclosed scope-narrowing, not a factual error — but IVT, asymptotes, and algebraic limit techniques are absent.
issues.fix: No math fix required; optionally tighten the title or overview to set expectation that asymptotes, IVT, and algebraic evaluation come in later videos.

notes: Mathematically sound throughout with correct definitions, notation, the three-condition continuity test, and matching pause-question answers; only minor AP-convention imprecisions (infinite-limit DNE phrasing and the omitted oscillating discontinuity type) warrant attention.
